executive_brief: The threat landscape has fundamentally shifted from opportunistic exploitation to machine-speed, autonomous attack chains. Artificial intelligence has compressed the vulnerability-to-exploitation window from months to hours, rendering traditional patching cycles obsolete. India's CERT-In now mandates a 12-hour remediation window for internet-facing flaws after AI tools autonomously chained legacy vulnerabilities into functional exploits [111, 177, 164]. Simultaneously, Anthropic's Project Glasswing identified over 10,000 high-severity flaws in open-source infrastructure, exposing a critical bottleneck: discovery now outpaces human remediation capacity [65, 112, 75]. Defenders operating on 30-day patch SLAs are already compromised.

threat_stories.headline: The AI Exploitation Arms Race: Negative TTE and the 12-Hour Patching Mandate
threat_stories.narrative:
Artificial intelligence has industrialized exploit development, collapsing the mean time to exploit (TTE) into negative territory where weaponization precedes patch release [58, 76]. On May 23, a cluster of Linux kernel vulnerabilities (Dirty Frag, Copy Fail, Fragnesia) abusing the page cache abstraction achieved local privilege escalation across multiple distributions within hours of disclosure [58]. By May 27, Anthropic's Claude Mythos Preview autonomously discovered and exploited a 17-year-old FreeBSD NFS remote code execution flaw (CVE-2026-4747) from a single prompt, demonstrating that AI agents now operate as independent security principals capable of chaining complex attack paths without human direction [164].

This acceleration forced regulatory intervention. On May 26, CERT-In issued an emergency directive mandating that organizations patch, mitigate, or isolate known exploited internet-facing vulnerabilities within 12 hours [111, 177]. The mandate acknowledges that AI-assisted reconnaissance and automated exploitation have eliminated the grace period defenders previously relied upon. Enterprises clinging to quarterly vulnerability scans or CVSS-driven prioritization are operating in a blind spot. The window for reactive defense has closed; continuous threat exposure management and automated containment are now baseline requirements.

timeline.event: Disclosure of Dirty Frag, Copy Fail, and Fragnesia Linux kernel LPE vulnerabilities
timeline.significance: Demonstrates AI-accelerated discovery compressing TTE to negative values

timeline.event: Project Glasswing announces identification of 10,000+ high/critical OSS vulnerabilities
timeline.significance: Reveals systemic bottleneck: AI discovery outpaces human patching capacity

timeline.event: CERT-In issues 12-hour patching mandate for internet-facing crown jewels
timeline.significance: Regulatory acknowledgment that traditional patch SLAs are obsolete against AI-driven exploitation

timeline.event: Claude Mythos autonomously exploits 17-year-old FreeBSD NFS RCE (CVE-2026-4747)
timeline.significance: Confirms AI agents can weaponize legacy flaws without predefined signatures or human guidance

threat_stories.impact_assessment: Global enterprise infrastructure, cloud-native deployments, and open-source dependency chains. Organizations with unpatched internet-facing assets face immediate compromise. Linux-based servers and FreeBSD-dependent services experience elevated risk of local privilege escalation and remote code execution.
threat_stories.action_required: Implement Continuous Threat Exposure Management (CTEM) to prioritize remediation by real exploitability rather than CVSS scores. Deploy automated network isolation and WAF rules for critical internet-facing assets. Enforce 12-hour containment SLAs for CISA KEV catalog entries. Integrate AI-assisted code auditing into CI/CD pipelines to accelerate patch validation.

threat_stories.headline: The Developer Supply Chain Bloodbath: Megalodon, TanStack, and CI/CD Poisoning
threat_stories.narrative:
The open-source developer ecosystem has become a primary attack surface, with threat actors executing coordinated supply chain poisonings across GitHub, npm, and VSCode. The Megalodon campaign (attributed to build-bot) compromised over 5,500 public repositories in a single six-hour window on May 22 by abusing stolen Personal Access Tokens to inject base64-encoded bash payloads directly into GitHub Actions workflow files [21, 28, 123]. These malicious workflows, disguised as SysDiag and Optimize-Build triggers, executed during CI/CD runs to harvest cloud credentials, SSH keys, and OIDC tokens, exfiltrating them to a dedicated C2 server at 216.126.225.129:8443 [123].

The attack vector expanded rapidly. On May 26, attackers executed a Git tag poisoning campaign against Laravel-Lang Composer packages, rewriting over 700 historical tags to point to malicious forks that deployed a cross-platform PHP information stealer upon Composer installation [130, 100]. By May 28, CISA added three additional supply chain compromises to the Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog: trojanized DAEMON Tools Lite installers, 42 poisoned @tanstack npm packages leveraging cache poisoning and OIDC token theft, and a malicious Nx Console VSCode extension (v18.95.0) [246]. The campaign demonstrates a mature, automated pipeline for infiltrating developer environments and pivoting into enterprise infrastructure.

timeline.event: Megalodon campaign injects malicious GitHub Actions workflows into 5,500+ repositories
timeline.significance: Demonstrates large-scale CI/CD secret exfiltration via compromised PATs

timeline.event: GlassWorm botnet infrastructure disrupted by CrowdStrike and Google
timeline.significance: Temporary disruption of trojanized package distribution, but supply chain vector remains active

timeline.event: Laravel-Lang Composer packages poisoned via Git tag rewriting
timeline.significance: Cross-platform PHP stealer deployed to developer workstations and CI runners

timeline.event: CISA adds TanStack, DAEMON Tools, and Nx Console flaws to KEV catalog
timeline.significance: Confirms active exploitation of IDE extensions and npm packages via cache poisoning and OIDC theft

threat_stories.impact_assessment: Software development teams, CI/CD pipelines, cloud infrastructure, and enterprise environments relying on compromised open-source dependencies. Credential theft leads to lateral movement into production environments.
threat_stories.action_required: Immediately rotate all PATs, deploy keys, and CI/CD secrets. Audit GitHub Actions workflow files for unauthorized base64-encoded bash scripts. Enforce branch protection rules requiring pull requests for workflow changes. Implement strict dependency verification, pin package versions to known clean commits, and isolate developer environments from production networks.

threat_stories.headline: Identity Bypass Industrialized: Kali365, OAuth Abuse, and the Death of Push MFA
threat_stories.narrative:
Multifactor authentication has been systematically neutralized by industrialized phishing-as-a-service platforms. The Kali365 ecosystem leverages the Microsoft OAuth device code flow to completely bypass MFA controls. Victims are tricked into entering legitimate device codes on spoofed landing pages, granting attackers valid OAuth access and refresh tokens that authenticate directly to Microsoft 365 without triggering MFA challenges [108, 143, 149]. Attackers then register trusted devices, create malicious inbox rules to suppress security alerts, and maintain persistent access indefinitely [108].

Concurrently, Chinese-language PhaaS platforms have weaponized real-time OTP interception. Using AI-generated phishing pages deployed via Puppeteer, attackers capture credentials and simultaneously trigger OTP requests on their own devices, relaying the codes seconds before expiration [71, 257]. Delivery occurs through encrypted messaging channels like RCS and iMessage, evading carrier-side filtering [71]. On May 26, security experts confirmed that push-based MFA and SMS/OTP are no longer viable defenses against organized threat actors [108, 136]. The identity layer has become the primary attack surface, with valid tokens rendering traditional endpoint detection and network segmentation ineffective [1].

timeline.event: FBI warns of Kali365 phishing service targeting Microsoft 365 accounts
timeline.significance: Highlights large-scale OAuth device code abuse bypassing MFA

timeline.event: GTIG publishes analysis on Chinese-language PhaaS real-time OTP interception
timeline.significance: Confirms AI-generated phishing and encrypted messaging delivery neutralize SMS/OTP MFA

timeline.event: Security experts declare push MFA obsolete against modern threat actors
timeline.significance: Industry consensus shifts to phishing-resistant authentication as mandatory baseline

timeline.event: FBI warns of Kali365 phishing kit breaking into M365 accounts without passwords
timeline.significance: Reinforces OAuth token theft as the dominant initial access vector

threat_stories.impact_assessment: Enterprise cloud environments, Microsoft 365 tenants, financial services, and any organization relying on SMS, voice, or push-based MFA. Compromised OAuth tokens enable persistent, undetectable lateral movement.
threat_stories.action_required: Immediately block or tightly restrict Microsoft OAuth device code authentication flows via Conditional Access policies. Transition all critical accounts to FIDO2/WebAuthn phishing-resistant credentials. Implement continuous access evaluation, monitor for unauthorized device registrations, and revoke stale OAuth tokens. Deploy behavioral analytics to detect anomalous login patterns and rapid OTP usage.

threat_stories.headline: Critical Enterprise Software Under Active Siege: Drupal, FortiClient, and LiteSpeed
threat_stories.narrative:
A synchronized wave of actively exploited zero-day and n-day vulnerabilities has targeted foundational enterprise software. On May 22, Drupal maintainers disclosed CVE-2026-9082, a critical SQL injection flaw in the database query sanitization API affecting PostgreSQL-backed installations [35, 53]. Automated exploitation campaigns began within 48 hours, targeting thousands of websites globally. CISA added the flaw to the KEV catalog on May 24, mandating federal remediation by May 27 [61, 101].

Simultaneously, threat actors exploited CVE-2026-35616, an authentication bypass in FortiClient Endpoint Management Server (EMS) [254, 263]. Attackers bypassed API authentication, gained administrative access, and pushed malicious PowerShell commands disguised as legitimate Fortinet updates, deploying the EKZ Infostealer across managed endpoints [253, 254]. On May 23, CISA flagged CVE-2026-48172 in the LiteSpeed cPanel plugin, which allows arbitrary script execution with root privileges via improper Redis function handling [47, 213]. Federal agencies were given a four-day deadline to patch [167]. These vulnerabilities share a common trait: unauthenticated remote access that enables immediate privilege escalation and credential theft.

timeline.event: Drupal CVE-2026-9082 disclosed; automated exploitation begins
timeline.significance: Rapid weaponization of SQLi flaw targeting PostgreSQL deployments

timeline.event: LiteSpeed cPanel CVE-2026-48172 added to KEV; active exploitation confirmed
timeline.significance: Root-level script execution via cPanel user accounts

timeline.event: CISA mandates federal patching for Drupal SQLi by May 27
timeline.significance: Regulatory enforcement due to widespread automated scanning

timeline.event: FortiClient EMS CVE-2026-35616 actively exploited to deploy EKZ Infostealer
timeline.significance: Trusted endpoint management infrastructure weaponized for credential theft

threat_stories.impact_assessment: Web applications running Drupal, enterprises using FortiClient EMS for endpoint management, and hosting providers utilizing LiteSpeed cPanel. Full system compromise, credential exfiltration, and persistent remote access.
threat_stories.action_required: Immediately patch Drupal to the latest version, FortiClient EMS to 7.4.7, and LiteSpeed cPanel to 2.4.7+. Deploy WAF rules to block SQL injection and malicious deserialization payloads. Restrict EMS and cPanel management interfaces to trusted IP ranges. Monitor for anomalous PowerShell execution and unauthorized credential harvesting.

threat_stories.headline: The Physical Extortion Playbook: Silent Ransom Group and In-Person Data Theft
threat_stories.narrative:
Cybercriminals have abandoned purely digital intrusion in favor of physical access campaigns that bypass network defenses entirely. The Silent Ransom Group (SRG) now dispatches operatives to target locations, impersonating IT support to gain physical access and insert malicious USB drives [153, 174, 237]. Employees, deceived by fabricated technical issues, voluntarily connect the devices, which install remote access tools, keyloggers, and data exfiltration utilities like WinSCP and Rclone [237]. The stolen data is weaponized for pure extortion, with groups like ShinyHunters demanding payment without deploying ransomware encryption [62, 215].

This tactic proved devastating against Carnival Corporation in late April 2026. SRG operatives socially engineered an employee into granting internal system access, exfiltrating personal data for nearly 6 million individuals before the breach was disclosed on May 28 [215, 219, 250]. The FBI issued explicit warnings on May 27 and 28, detailing how extortion crews visit law firms and corporate offices, exploiting inadequate physical security controls and unverified IT support request workflows [190, 207, 237]. The attack chain relies entirely on human trust and physical access gaps, rendering traditional cybersecurity controls irrelevant.

timeline.event: ShinyHunters extortion campaigns increase; pure extortion model replaces ransomware
timeline.significance: Shift toward data theft and public leak threats without system encryption

timeline.event: FBI warns of extortion hackers visiting US law firms to steal data via USB drives
timeline.significance: Highlights physical access campaigns bypassing network perimeters

timeline.event: FBI details Silent Ransom Group tactics; Carnival confirms 6M-record breach
timeline.significance: Confirms social engineering and physical USB drops as primary initial access vectors for major breaches

threat_stories.impact_assessment: Law firms, corporate headquarters, healthcare providers, and any organization with inadequate physical security or unverified IT support protocols. Complete data exfiltration and regulatory/financial extortion.
threat_stories.action_required: Enforce strict physical access controls, including visitor verification and ID checks. Disable USB ports on employee workstations and enforce device control policies. Mandate out-of-band verification for all IT support requests. Deploy EDR solutions to monitor for unauthorized remote access tool installations and anomalous data transfers via WinSCP/Rclone.

actor_profiles.name: Kali365 Operators
actor_profiles.motivation: Financial (Credential theft, MFA bypass, account takeover)
actor_profiles.recent_activity: Deployed large-scale OAuth device code phishing campaigns targeting Microsoft 365. Bypassed MFA by harvesting valid access/refresh tokens, registering trusted devices, and suppressing security alerts via malicious inbox rules.

actor_profiles.name: Megalodon (build-bot)
actor_profiles.motivation: Financial (CI/CD secret exfiltration, supply chain poisoning)
actor_profiles.recent_activity: Compromised over 5,500 GitHub repositories by abusing stolen PATs to inject base64-encoded bash payloads into GitHub Actions workflows. Harvested cloud credentials, SSH keys, and OIDC tokens during CI/CD execution.

actor_profiles.name: Silent Ransom Group
actor_profiles.recent_activity: Dispatched operatives to target locations impersonating IT support. Used social engineering to gain physical access, inserted malicious USB drives, and exfiltrated data using legitimate tools (WinSCP, Rclone). Executed pure extortion campaigns without ransomware deployment.

actor_profiles.name: Nimbus Manticore
actor_profiles.recent_activity: Expanded campaigns targeting aviation and software companies using AI-assisted malware (MiniFast, MiniJunk V2). Deployed trojanized Zoom installers and leveraged SEO poisoning targeting fake Oracle SQL Developer downloads. Utilized .NET AppDomain hijacking for persistence.

actor_profiles.name: ShinyHunters
actor_profiles.recent_activity: Executed social engineering campaigns to compromise employee credentials at Carnival Corporation, exfiltrating nearly 6 million records. Shifted to pure extortion model, threatening public data leaks without deploying ransomware encryption.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: SQL injection flaw in the database query sanitization API allowing unauthenticated attackers to inject arbitrary SQL commands on PostgreSQL-backed installations. Enables information disclosure, privilege escalation, and remote code execution.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Apply the critical security patch released by Drupal on May 20, 2026 immediately. Federal agencies must remediate by May 27, 2026.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Improper access control allowing unauthenticated attackers to bypass authentication via API endpoints. Crafted HTTP requests trigger privilege escalation and remote code execution, enabling deployment of credential-stealing malware disguised as legitimate updates.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Install hotfixes for EMS 7.4.5/7.4.6 immediately, or upgrade to version 7.4.7. Restrict console access to trusted administrative IP ranges.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Incorrect privilege assignment in the Redis enable/disable function allowing any cPanel user to execute arbitrary scripts with root privileges via improper handling of lsws.redisAble.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Upgrade to version 2.4.7 or later. Monitor cPanel logs for suspicious Redis-related API calls and block unauthorized IPs.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: SQL injection vulnerability in the Content API allowing unauthenticated attackers to read database contents, steal Admin API keys, and inject malicious JavaScript payloads for ClickFix ad-fraud campaigns.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Update Ghost CMS to the latest patched version immediately. Rotate all compromised Admin API keys and remove injected scripts from the database.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Hardcoded ASP.NET machineKey values enabling ViewState deserialization attacks. Attackers craft malicious __VIEWSTATE payloads to achieve unauthenticated remote code execution and deploy Godzilla web shells.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Generate and deploy unique, cryptographically strong machine keys for each instance. Remove hardcoded keys from web.config and apply vendor patches.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Directory traversal vulnerability allowing remote attackers to traverse directory structures outside the web root. Enables unauthorized access to sensitive files, configuration data, and credentials.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Apply the official security patch released by TrendAI immediately. Restrict network access to the management console and deploy WAF rules to block traversal payloads.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Windows zero-day vulnerability (YellowKey) publicly disclosed without vendor coordination. Working POC exploit code published on GitHub. Rapid weaponization observed alongside BlueHammer and RedSun flaws.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Apply Microsoft security updates immediately upon release. Implement strict network segmentation and application whitelisting to limit exploit propagation.

critical_vulnerabilities.description: Improperly authorized linked-device sync messages allowing attackers to parse content from arbitrary URLs. Combined with CVE-2025-43300, enables zero-click account takeover and cryptographic material exfiltration without triggering notifications.
critical_vulnerabilities.remediation: Update iOS to version 16.7.12 or later. Update WhatsApp to the latest version. Reinstall WhatsApp and complete fresh authentication to evict attacker sessions.

hunting_leads.title: GitHub Actions CI/CD Poisoning (Megalodon)
hunting_leads.context: Attackers inject base64-encoded bash payloads into workflow files via compromised PATs. These execute during CI/CD runs to exfiltrate cloud credentials and OIDC tokens.
hunting_leads.query: source="github_audit_log" action="workflow_run" | where workflow_name IN ("SysDiag", "Optimize-Build") | eval payload=base64_decode(inputs) | where payload LIKE "%curl%" OR payload LIKE "%wget%" OR payload LIKE "%216.126.225.129%"

hunting_leads.title: OAuth Device Code Phishing (Kali365)
hunting_leads.context: Victims enter legitimate Microsoft OAuth device codes on spoofed pages. Attackers harvest access/refresh tokens, bypassing MFA and registering trusted devices.
hunting_leads.query: source="azure_signin_logs" | where app_id IN ("d3590ed6-52b3-4102-aeff-aad2292ab01c", "00000002-0000-0ff1-ce00-000000000000") | where auth_method == "device_code" | where risk_level != "none" | stats count by user_principal_name, ip_address
indicators: New device registration from anomalous IPs
OAuth token refresh from unfamiliar locations

hunting_leads.title: FortiClient EMS Malicious Configuration Push
hunting_leads.context: Compromised EMS servers push malicious PowerShell commands disguised as legitimate Fortinet endpoint updates to managed devices.
hunting_leads.query: source="fortinet_ems_logs" | where action="policy_push" OR action="config_update" | where payload CONTAINS "powershell" OR payload CONTAINS "invoke-webrequest" OR payload CONTAINS "EKZ" | stats count by target_endpoint, admin_user

hunting_leads.title: Linux Kernel LPE (Dirty Frag / Copy Fail)
hunting_leads.context: AI-discovered page cache abstraction flaws enable local privilege escalation. Exploits often run as untrusted local users before patch deployment.
hunting_leads.query: source="syslog" OR source="auditd" | where process_name IN ("dirty_frag", "copy_fail", "fragnesia", "exploit") | where action="execve" | where user_id != 0 AND process_user_id != 0 | stats count by host, user_id, process_name
indicators: Execution of untrusted binaries in /tmp or /var/tmp
Kernel panic or OOM killer events post-execution

hunting_leads.title: Physical USB Data Exfiltration (Silent Ransom Group)
hunting_leads.context: Operatives insert malicious USB drives into employee workstations. Devices install RATs and exfiltrate data via WinSCP/Rclone to external IPs.
hunting_leads.query: source="edr_endpoint_logs" | where event_type="usb_device_connected" | join [source="process_creation" | where parent_process_name IN ("WinSCP.exe", "rclone.exe", "AnyDesk.exe", "RustDesk.exe")] on host_id | stats count by host_id, usb_serial, user
indicators: Remote access tool installation post-USB insert
Large outbound data transfers via WinSCP/Rclone
Unfamiliar IP connections to cloud storage endpoints

top_vulnerabilities: {'summary': 'Identity and Access Management (IAM) architectural weaknesses, including credential/session management flaws, MFA bypass vulnerabilities, and excessive privilege configurations.', 'severity': 'high', 'affected_products': ['Cloud identity providers', 'Single Sign-On (SSO) platforms', 'OAuth/OIDC implementations', 'SaaS collaboration platforms'], 'complexity': 'low', 'cvss_score': '', 'workarounds': ['Deploy MFA phishing-resistant methods to counter fatigue and AiTM attacks', 'Bind sessions to device context to prevent token replay', 'Restrict and continuously audit OAuth application consent grants', 'Implement separation of duties and enforced verification in critical business workflows'], 'references': ['CSO Online: Identity as the primary attack surface: What modern breaches are really exploiting (2026-05-22)'], 'integrity_impact': 'high', 'description': 'Modern threat actors increasingly target identity systems rather than traditional network perimeters. Initial access is achieved through credential stuffing, OAuth consent phishing, Adversary-in-the-Middle (AiTM) frameworks, and MFA fatigue attacks. These techniques exploit weaknesses in how identities are verified and sessions are managed, often bypassing password-centric and push-based MFA controls. Once authenticated, attackers leverage poorly monitored service accounts, excessive privileges, and misconfigured role assignments to move laterally and establish persistence. Because the activity uses valid credentials and tokens, it appears as legitimate user behavior to traditional security controls, allowing breaches to persist undetected.', 'attack_vector': 'network', 'privileges_required': 'none', 'exploit_maturity': 'high', 'availability_impact': 'high', 'remediation': 'Implement phishing-resistant, hardware-backed credentials (FIDO2/WebAuthn). Enforce conditional access policies based on contextual risk signals. Adopt just-in-time (JIT) access models and approval-based privilege elevation. Conduct continuous access reviews to eliminate privilege debt. Strictly enforce the principle of least privilege (POLP) across all user and service accounts.', 'exploit_available': 'yes', 'cve_id': '', 'cvss_vector': '', 'user_interaction': 'required', 'scope': 'changed', 'confidentiality_impact': 'high', 'published_date': '', 'discovery_date': ''}

{'summary': 'Directory traversal vulnerability in the on-premise version of TrendAI Apex One allowing unauthorized file access.', 'severity': 'high', 'affected_products': ['TrendAI Apex One'], 'complexity': 'low', 'cvss_score': 9.8, 'workarounds': ['Restrict network access to the Apex One management console to trusted IP ranges only.', 'Deploy Web Application Firewall (WAF) rules to block common directory traversal payloads (e.g., ../, %2e%2e%2f).', 'Disable or isolate the vulnerable service if patching is not immediately feasible.'], 'references': ['https://www.securityweek.com/trendai-patches-apex-one-zero-day-exploited-in-the-wild'], 'integrity_impact': 'high', 'description': 'CVE-2026-34926 is a directory traversal flaw affecting the on-premise deployment of TrendAI Apex One. statistics.key_changes: AI-driven exploitation has compressed vulnerability-to-compromise timelines to under 12 hours, forcing regulatory mandates (CERT-In) and rendering traditional 30-day patch SLAs obsolete. Supply chain attacks now target CI/CD pipelines and IDE extensions at scale, while physical extortion campaigns bypass network defenses entirely.
emerging_trends: AI-assisted autonomous exploit chaining and negative TTE environments
OAuth device code abuse replacing traditional credential phishing
Git tag poisoning and cache poisoning in package registries (npm, Composer)
Physical access campaigns using USB drops and IT support impersonation
Pure extortion models replacing ransomware encryption
declining_threats: Push-based and SMS/OTP multifactor authentication (rapidly bypassed)
Signature-based phishing detection (neutralized by AI-generated pages)
Traditional perimeter-focused vulnerability scanning (outpaced by AI reconnaissance)
Ransomware deployment as primary monetization (shifted to data extortion)
